candidate_targets.label: meson two-point correlator with unspecified channel/operator
candidate_targets.summary: The request asks for a meson correlator but does not identify the channel or operator.

candidate_targets.label: pion two-point correlator
candidate_targets.summary: Pion is the most likely meson channel for the current grounded workflows, but it is not confirmed by the request.

candidate_targets.label: meson spectroscopy correlator
candidate_targets.summary: If the intent is a small meson-spectroscopy code path with fixed gamma insertions, the grounded meson-spec workflow is another plausible candidate.

candidate_targets.label: pion dispersion correlator
candidate_targets.summary: If the user actually intends a momentum-projected pion study, the supported narrow dispersion workflow is another plausible candidate.

external_citations.chosen_convention: Use a Euclidean two-point correlator with interpolating operators chosen to overlap the target pion channel at zero momentum.
external_citations.knowledge_boundary_note: This citation record comes from a local curated JSON artifact, not from a live online lookup during the current run.
external_citations.title: Hadron Spectroscopy with Lattice QCD
external_citations.why_needed: PyQUDA examples and tests show the implementation pattern, but this review provides the lattice-spectroscopy framing for the correlator and operator-selection convention.

external_citations.chosen_convention: Use the narrow wall-source zero-momentum convention and record that the p=0 truncation corresponds to wall sources.
external_citations.knowledge_boundary_note: This citation record comes from a local curated JSON artifact, not from a live online lookup during the current run.
external_citations.title: The sigma meson from lattice QCD with two-pion interpolating operators
external_citations.why_needed: PyQUDA local references encode wall inversions and zero-momentum contractions but do not explain the physics-side meaning of the wall-source p=0 choice.

external_lookup.reason: External lookup is disabled by default. Re-run with --enable-external-lookup to allow live online lookup.

formula_proposals.convention: Use the canonical pseudoscalar pion interpolating operator and the zero-momentum Euclidean two-point correlator.
formula_proposals.label: Pion pseudoscalar two-point correlator

citations.chosen_convention: Use a momentum-projected Euclidean pion two-point correlator with a fixed explicit momentum list to support a narrow dispersion workflow.
citations.knowledge_boundary_note: This citation record comes from a local curated JSON artifact, not from a live online lookup during the current run.
citations.title: Hadron Spectroscopy with Lattice QCD
citations.why_needed: The local PyQUDA example provides the implementation path, while this curated review record documents the physics-side interpretation of momentum-projected two-point correlators.

formula_proposals.convention: Use the canonical pseudoscalar pion interpolating operator together with an explicit finite momentum list to build a narrow pion-dispersion workflow.
formula_proposals.correlator: C_\pi(t,\vec p) = \sum_{\vec x} e^{i\vec p\cdot\vec x}\langle O_\pi(\vec x,t) O_\pi^\dagger(\vec 0,0) \rangle
formula_proposals.label: Pion momentum-projected two-point correlator for dispersion analysis

citations.chosen_convention: Use a narrow meson-spectroscopy two-point workflow with fixed wall-source bilinears and a grounded finite-momentum family.
citations.knowledge_boundary_note: This citation record comes from a local curated JSON artifact, not from a live online lookup during the current run.
citations.title: Hadron Spectroscopy with Lattice QCD
citations.why_needed: The local PyQUDA mesonspec test provides the implementation path, while this curated review record documents the spectroscopy-side interpretation of the correlator family.

formula_proposals.convention: Use the fixed wall-source meson-spectroscopy path from the local PyQUDA meson-spec test, including the grounded momentum family with |p|^2 <= 9.
formula_proposals.correlator: C_{\gamma_5}(t,\vec p) = \sum_{\vec x} e^{i\vec p\cdot\vec x}\langle O_{\gamma_5}(\vec x,t) O_{\gamma_5}^\dagger(\vec 0,0) \rangle
formula_proposals.label: Meson spectroscopy correlator with pseudoscalar insertion

formula_proposals.convention: Keep the same local meson-spec workflow, but expose the second fixed gamma-insertion channel already present in the upstream test path.
formula_proposals.correlator: C_{\gamma_4\gamma_5}(t,\vec p) = \sum_{\vec x} e^{i\vec p\cdot\vec x}\langle O_{\gamma_4\gamma_5}(\vec x,t) O_{\gamma_4\gamma_5}^\dagger(\vec 0,0) \rangle
formula_proposals.label: Meson spectroscopy correlator with temporal-axial insertion
